司令官か。勘違いするなよ、<br>別に、店にクレームを入れているわけじゃない。:
Boss. Don't get the wrong idea—I'm not lodging a complaint against
the shop.
この新兵が、価値のない剣を掴まされそうになっていてな。<br>見ていられなかった:
This rookie was about to be stuck with a worthless sword. I just
couldn't stand by and watch.
店主、この剣はなかなかの業物だ。だが、重心が少し柄に近い。<br>これでは、使い:
Shopkeeper, this sword is a fine blade. But the center of gravity is a
bit close to the hilt. That means it'll be picky about its user.
まだ戦いに不慣れなこの新兵には、こっちの安い剣の方が扱いやすいはずだ。<br>お:
For this rookie inexperienced in battle, this cheaper sword should be
easier to handle. Come on, try it yourself. See? I'm right, aren't I?
どんな名剣でも、扱うことが出来なければただのガラクタだ。<br>本当のモノの価値:
No matter how famous a sword is, if you can't wield it, it's just junk.
That's what true value means.
